stages.description: Molecule exists as persistent excitation in bounded phase space
stages.narrative: The ion (m/z = 1333.90, z = 3) has neutral mass 3998.69 Da. As a persistent excitation in bounded phase space (Axiom 1), it must oscillate (Thm 2.3) at rest frequency omega_0 = 5.6589e+27 rad/s. The frequency-energy identity gives E = hbar*omega_0 = 3.7248e+12 eV. At principal depth n = 4, the partition capacity is C(4) = 32 discrete modes.

theorems.description: All persistent systems occupy bounded phase space
theorems.detail: Phase space volume = 3.970e-90 m^6*kg^3/s^3, r_mol = 3.42 nm, v_th = 43.3 m/s

theorems.description: Bounded persistent systems must oscillate
theorems.detail: Rest frequency omega_0 = 5.6589e+27 rad/s, period = 1.1103e-27 s

theorems.description: E = hbar * omega for fundamental mode
Discrete modes from Koopman operator
theorems.detail: n = 4, C(n) = 2*4^2 = 32 modes, cumulative N = 60

stages.description: Sample enters instrument: massive volume compression begins
stages.narrative: Injection compresses the sample from 1 uL to single-ion detection volume ~3 nm^3, a 3.3e+20x compression. The Compression Theorem (Thm 5.6) gives a cost of 47.3 k_BT (68 bits of spatial information erased). This is consistent with the Landauer bound.

theorems.description: Volume reduction costs divergent distinguishability energy
theorems.detail: Compression 3.33e+20x, cost = 47.3 k_BT (68 bits erased), Landauer minimum = 47.3 k_BT

theorems.description: Partition depth bounded by phase space limits
theorems.detail: Depth bounds: M_min = 0.631, M_max = 43.0 (range = 42.4)

stages.description: Column traversal as partition lag through trap array
stages.narrative: The chromatographic column (L=15.0 cm, d_p=1.7 um) contains 88,235 trap sites. Each site performs one partition operation in time tau_p >= hbar/(k_BT) = 2.4392e-14 s. The retention time t_R = 350.2 min IS the partition lag (Eq. 13.2). The maximum propagation speed through partition space is c_derived = 2.9979e+08 m/s (ratio to c: 1.0000). The categorical temperature at each trap is T_cat = 3.1315e+02 K - this is an information temperature, not kinetic.

theorems.description: t_R = tau_p(S_k, S_t, S_e): chromatographic retention IS partition lag
theorems.detail: Column has 88,235 trap sites (L=15.0 cm, d_p=1.7 um). Min partition lag per trap = 2.4392e-14 s. RT = 350.2 min = 88235 partition operations.

theorems.description: Finite maximum speed c = v_Bohr/alpha for partition state changes
theorems.detail: v_Bohr = a_0*omega = 2.1877e+06 m/s, alpha = 0.007297, c = v_Bohr/alpha = 2.9979e+08 m/s (ratio to c: 1.000000). Column effective speed: 0.0000 m/s.

theorems.description: T_cat = hbar*omega/(2*pi*k_B) - information temperature, not kinetic
theorems.detail: Categorical temperature T_cat = 3.1315e+02 K (kinetic T = 313.1 K, ratio = 1.0000e+00)

stages.narrative: Ionization via ESI creates a partition malformation: z=3 electrons removed from Z~1999. The Composition Theorem gives delta_M = 4.830 (binding reduces depth). Compression cost of ionization: 0.62 eV. Conservation: M_neutral -> M_ion + M_electrons + M_photon. Charge EMERGES from partitioning: 3 partition levels predict z=3, observed z=3. Partition coordinates: (n,l,m,s) = (4,3,2,0.5), C(4) = 32.

theorems.description: Binding reduces total partition depth: M_bound < M_free
theorems.detail: M_free (pep+gly separate) = 8.557, M_bound (glycopeptide) = 3.727, delta_M = 4.830 (binding energy as depth reduction)

theorems.description: Ionization energy as partition compression cost
theorems.detail: Z ~ 1999 electrons, removing z=3: IE_partition ~ z*k_BT*ln(Z) = 0.62 eV

theorems.description: Total partition depth conserved: M_neutral = M_ion + M_e + M_photon
Charge exists only for partitioned entities; z = partition levels
theorems.detail: Charge emerges from partitioning: 3 partition levels (peptide + glycan + mods) -> z_pred = 3, z_obs = 3. Method: ESI

theorems.description: Transport vanishes when partition undefined (not applicable here)

value.note: Would apply to superconducting ion guides at T < T_c

theorems.detail: Extinction theorem not directly applicable to ionization. Would apply if ion guide cooled below T_c (superconducting regime).

theorems.description: Ion maps to valid (n, l, m, s) with 0 <= l < n, |m| <= l
Ion is incomplete categorical structure seeking depth minimization
theorems.detail: Ion with z=3 is a partition malformation: 3 missing electron(s) = unfilled partition states. Thermodynamically driven toward completion.

stages.description: Ion dynamics in ORBITRAP analyzer from partition Lagrangian
stages.narrative: The ion enters the ORBITRAP analyzer with partition inertia mu = alpha*(m/z) = 3.5488e-43 kg. The Lagrangian L = 5.0000e+01 J. The specific analyzer equation follows from the partition Lagrangian with zero adjustable parameters. {'omega_orbi_rad_s': 1.678642172745722e+24, 'freq_orbi_Hz': 2.6716419947500095e+23, 'freq_orbi_kHz': 2.6716419947500093e+20, 'scaling': 'omega ~ sqrt(z/m)', 'kappa': 1000000.0}

theorems.description: omega_orbi = sqrt(kappa/mu) ~ sqrt(z/m)
Partition EOM has Lorentz structure: mu*x'' = -grad(M) + mu*v x B_M

value.interpretation: Lorentz force emerges from partition gradient descent

theorems.expected: Same structure as m*a = q(E + v x B)
theorems.detail: The partition EOM mu*x'' = -grad(M) + mu*v x B_M has the same structure as the Lorentz force. The Lorentz force is not fundamental - it emerges from partition depth minimization.

stages.description: Fragmentation via HCD - thermodynamics of a single ion
stages.narrative: In the collision cell (HCD, N2 at 2.0 mTorr): the single ion obeys PV = k_BT_cat with T_cat = 0.01 uK (information temperature, NOT kinetic). The ion undergoes ~0 collisions in ~83.1 us transit. Each collision IS one time step (fundamental identity). Fragmentation produces 199 peaks with Delta_S = 137.9 k_B (strictly > 0: partition permanence). Selection rules: 100.0% of transitions obey Delta_l = +/-1.

theorems.theorem_name: Single-Particle Ideal Gas Law (Ion Observatory Thm)
theorems.description: PV = k_B * T_cat for N=1: resolves single-particle thermodynamics
theorems.expected: T_cat = hbar*omega/(2*pi*k_B) > 0; PV = k_B*T_cat for single ion
theorems.detail: Single-ion ideal gas: omega_trap = 4.9122e+03 rad/s, T_cat = 0.01 uK (NOT kinetic T = 313 K). PV = 3.3507e-07 J.

theorems.description: Delta_S_cat > 0 strictly (not >= 0). Partitions cannot be deleted.
theorems.detail: Each of 199 fragment peaks represents a partition event. Minimum entropy: 199 * k_B*ln(2) = 137.9 k_B. This is STRICT inequality (> 0, not >= 0): partition permanence.

theorems.description: dM/dt = omega/(2*pi) = 1/<tau_p>: collision rate IS the rate of time

value.interpretation: Collisions ARE time passing, not events IN time

theorems.detail: In N2 at 2.0 mTorr: dM/dt = collision freq = 1.01e+04 Hz, expected 0.84 collisions in 83.1 us transit. The fundamental identity holds: dM/dt > 0.

theorems.description: Fragmentation irreversible: each break resolves infinite non-actualisations

value.note: Cannot un-fragment because residue cannot be recovered

theorems.expected: Arrow of time from partition asymmetry
theorems.detail: Each fragmentation creates 198 new partitions. Cannot reverse: partition permanence prevents residue recovery. This is categorical irreversibility, not statistical improbability.

theorems.description: Delta_l = +/-1, Delta_m in {0, +/-1}, Delta_s = 0
theorems.expected: All fragment transitions obey selection rules
theorems.detail: Selection rules (HCD): 198/198 transitions obey Delta_l = +/-1 (100.0% pass rate).

theorems.description: I(fragments) subset of I(precursor): fragments contained in parent
theorems.detail: Max fragment m/z = 1981.89, precursor m/z = 1333.90 (z=3). All fragments contained: True.

theorems.description: E_cm = E_col * m_gas / (m_ion + m_gas)
theorems.detail: CID: E_col = 30.0 eV, E_cm = 0.21 eV (N2), efficiency = 0.0070

stages.description: Fragment partition states measured: state counting and resolution limits
stages.narrative: MS2 analysis: 199 fragment peaks. Three-component decomposition: A=1.000, R=0.000, V=0.000 (sum=1.000000). Partition uncertainty: Delta_M*tau_p = 1.0546e-34 >= hbar. Resolution limit: 1.3663e-14 (R = 73187736626424) on ORBITRAP. Dispersion relation verified with residual 2.30e-16.

theorems.description: A + R + V = 1: actualized + residue + potential = constant
Delta_M * tau_p >= hbar
theorems.detail: Delta_M = 3.727, tau_p_min = 6.8317e-15 s, product = 1.0546e-34 J*s >= hbar = 1.0546e-34 J*s (ratio: 1.00)

theorems.description: [Delta(m/z)/(m/z)]_min = hbar / (T * Delta_M)
theorems.detail: Resolution limit = 1.3663e-14 (resolving power R = 73187736626424) for T = 0.5000 s on ORBITRAP.

theorems.description: omega^2 = omega_0^2 + c^2*k^2

stages.description: Reading the ion's memory: bijective transformation and mass = memory
stages.narrative:
Detection completes the journey. S-entropy: (0.8706, 1.0000, 1.0000). Bijective transformation: error = 1.11e-16 (zero information loss). Droplet: We=1506.875, Re=26894.241, Oh=0.001. Position-trajectory duality: the detected state IS the complete history. 

THE REVEAL: Mass = 3998.69 Da is accumulated non-actualisations - the record of everything that didn't happen while this ion maintained its identity. 96.3% of its rest energy (3.5868e+12 eV) is residue (memory). E = mc^2 = memory * c^2 is a THEOREM. The mass spectrometer reads memory. The spectrum is the ion's autobiography.

theorems.description: (Sk, St, Se) in [0,1]^3
theorems.detail: S-Entropy: (Sk, St, Se) = (0.8706, 1.0000, 1.0000), valid = True

theorems.description: Ion <-> S-Entropy <-> Droplet with zero information loss
theorems.detail: Bijective: error = 1.11e-16. Droplet: We = 1506.875, Re = 26894.241, Oh = 0.001.

theorems.description: We > 0, Re > 0, Oh > 0: physically meaningful droplet
theorems.detail: We = 1506.875 (capillary regime), Re = 26894.241 (laminar), Oh = 0.001 (viscous-dominated).

theorems.description: In partition space, position IS trajectory. Knowing what the ion IS tells you how it got there.

value.interpretation: The detected m/z, intensity pattern IS the complete partition trajectory of the ion

theorems.detail: The S-entropy coordinates (0.8706, 1.0000, 1.0000) encode both the ion's final state AND its complete partition history. This IS position-trajectory duality.

theorems.description: Mass is accumulated non-actualisations. The mass spectrometer reads memory.

value.interpretation: mass = accumulated record of non-actualisations = memory

theorems.expected: mass = accumulated residue from partition lag
theorems.detail: Mass = 3998.69 Da = 6.6400e-24 kg. Rest energy E_0 = 3.7248e+12 eV. Of this, 96.3% (3.5868e+12 eV) is accumulated residue (non-actualisations) = MASS. The remaining 3.7% is actualized structure. The mass spectrometer READS this memory.

theorems.description: E = mc^2: energy = memory * (max memory propagation rate)^2

value.c_meaning: maximum rate of partition state propagation

theorems.detail: E = mc^2 = 5.9677e-07 J = hbar*omega_0 = 5.9677e-07 J. This is a THEOREM, not a postulate. c is the max partition propagation speed. m is memory.

theorems.description: m_i = m_g: both derive from omega_0
theorems.detail: Both inertial mass (resistance to acceleration) and gravitational mass (coupling to gravity) equal hbar*omega_0/c^2 = 6.6400e-24 kg. Same omega_0 -> same mass. Geometric identity.

theorems.description: Cov(delta_Q, dS_cat) = 0: energy and memory are independent

value.interpretation: Energy can flow without creating distinctions, distinctions can be created without energy flow
value.resolves: Maxwell demon: demon manipulates heat, but entropy (memory) is independently conserved

theorems.expected: Energy and entropy are independent observables connected by c^2
theorems.detail: Heat-entropy decoupling: energy exchange and memory accumulation are algebraically independent. E = mc^2 is the conversion factor, not an identity. This resolves Maxwell's demon.
